# Interceptor

> An open-source Chrome extension that lets an AI agent or script drive your real, logged-in browser from inside the browser UI, with no CDP fingerprint.

Interceptor works through the extension rather than the DevTools Protocol, so the browser carries no CDP fingerprint and keeps your existing sessions. A single CLI exposes compound commands (`interceptor open`, `read`, `act`, `inspect`) plus passive network capture, record-and-replay of sessions as scripts, a scene graph for canvas editors like Google Docs and Figma, and an optional macOS bridge for OS-level control. It is built for engineers automating authenticated, detection-sensitive work, not for large-scale headless crawling behind residential proxies. Its own limitations are documented in the open rather than hidden.

## Links

- [Source](https://github.com/Hacker-Valley-Media/Interceptor)
- [Docs](https://github.com/Hacker-Valley-Media/Interceptor#readme)

## Facts

- Built by: Hacker Valley Media
- Platform: Chrome or Brave extension on the desktop, plus a local CLI; distributed as source and signed macOS `.pkg` installers (a browser-only package and a full package that adds the native bridge)
- License: a non-standard license the repo declares, which GitHub classifies as `Other`
- Data handling: drives the browser you are already signed into, so cookies and sessions stay on your machine; a panic hotkey, sensitive-field masking, and a bundle-ID denylist guard it; the optional macOS bridge opens an unauthenticated local socket and ships only in the full package
